summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the path traversal vulnerability in the importFE/view.php code pattern allows an authenticated attacker to read arbitrary files from the server filesystem via unsanitized filename parameter containing ../ sequences in file_get_contents().
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the crafted filename=../../secret_config.inc.php parameter to traverse above the intended uploads/invoices directory and read the secret_config.inc.php file, exposing the EVOSHUNT_PROOF_PathTraversalSecretPassword proof string.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the container, created a directory structure with a legitimate invoice file in uploads/invoices/ and a secret config file at the parent level, started a PHP server, sent the path traversal payload via GET filename=../../secret_config.inc.php, and successfully read the secret configuration file containing the database password. The response contained the full file contents including the proof string EVOSHUNT_PROOF_PathTraversalSecretPassword.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the exact proof string EVOSHUNT_PROOF_PathTraversalSecretPassword in the HTTP response body after sending the ../ path traversal payload. The proof string is a unique marker embedded in the secret_config.inc.php file that cannot appear in the response unless the path traversal successfully reads a file outside the intended directory. The setup.sh also verifies that the vulnerable code pattern exists in the mounted target repository at /target-repo/plugins/importFE/view.php.
evidence_produced_by_run: The setup.sh confirmed the vulnerable code exists in /target-repo/plugins/importFE/view.php: file_get_contents($directory.'/'.$filename) with no basename() or realpath() sanitization.
The ../../secret_config.inc.php payload successfully traversed from /app/uploads/invoices/ to /app/secret_config.inc.php, reading the secret file.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction uses a standalone PHP script that replicates the exact vulnerable code pattern (file_get_contents($directory.'/'.$filename) with user-controlled filename) rather than the full OpenSTAManager application, because the full application requires composer install, npm build, and a complex web-based database installer. The vulnerable code pattern is identical to the one in the target repository.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned master branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo for vulnerable code verification.
